output.action_summary: The chain begins at the Main Page, then links to the list of countries by population. This page links to the United Nations page, and the United Nations page links to UNICEF, fulfilling the requirement of a chain with at least 4 pages and showing adjacency pairs (e.g., the UN page links to UNICEF).

4f29699d-7b71-4675-9233-2d82e836f16a.title:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

b5290c8f-cd78-4c04-b7bc-a515347ce777.title: Build a chain of Wikipedia URLs

details.phase: Initial search and visit to build URL chain
details.plan: Begin by visiting https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page. Extract the list of hyperlinks that directly link to other Wikipedia pages. Select one URL from links on the main page that appears relevant. Visit that URL, then extract its links. Continue this process until a chain of at least 4 distinct Wikipedia URLs is formed. Each subsequent URL must be directly linked from the previous page, verifying the adjacency. After establishing 4 URLs, identify and explicitly verify at least one adjacency pair (URL[i] links to URL[i+1]) by checking the links on the page. Use search if needed to locate links or clarify page structures. Document the chain and the adjacency. The goal is to produce an ordered list of 4 Wikipedia URLs with citation of links, along with the adjacency verification.
